opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Parent calling on the patient's behalf) I feel completely drained, my stomach is cramping terribly, and I just can't stop throwing up. — Mum here: she's a type 1 diabetic, her sugar meter just says 'HI', and she's breathing really deep and fast. I'm scared.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: She's been throwing up for hours, is too weak to sit up, and her home blood-sugar reading is off the top of the meter.

symptom_narrative.onset: started feeling really unwell about a day ago; the vomiting and stomach cramps ramped up over the last 8 hours
symptom_narrative.character: profound tiredness and weakness, cramping tummy pain all over, and repeated vomiting — she has thrown up several times
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says she's never felt this wiped out; she can barely stay sitting up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the tummy pain is all over, not in one spot
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it keeps getting worse no matter what; she can't keep any fluids down
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she's been weeing huge amounts and drinking litres of water — went through more than 8 litres yesterday. Mum says she's gone very drowsy and is taking deep, fast breaths, and there's an odd sweet smell on her breath. She had a bit of a cold a couple of days ago.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound, very drowsy, struggling to hold herself upright

past_medical_history.how_managed: insulin pens (a background one and one with meals)

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: had a mild head-cold starting two days ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: adolescent living with parents; parent is present and made the call. Missed some insulin doses yesterday because she wasn't eating and felt too sick.

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home glucometer reading only; 'HI' means it exceeds the meter's measurable limit rather than a specific number

objective_data_offered.value: there's an odd sweet, fruity smell on her breath
she looks drained and very drowsy, her eyes look sunken
about four vomits in the last several hours, greenish
lots of empty water bottles around the bed; insulin pens scattered on the bedside table
